test_cases.description: Job relocation announcement — COO facing worker-sympathetic economics journalist, print format
test_cases.input_summary: Bridgeway Logistics COO; 120 Manchester jobs relocating; Robert Carver, National Tribune; worker-sympathetic correspondent; 3 key messages; severance figures and Eastern European costs off-limits; print format
test_cases.notes: Interview context paragraph correctly identified journalist's likely framing (worker-focused, cost-cutting angle). Key messages stated in one sentence each with rationale. Anticipated questions are adversarial as required. Every topic-to-avoid has bridging language. Closing reminder is a single, memorable, usable sentence. Document is scannable at 587 words.

test_cases.description: Product recall interview — CEO on radio, 10-minute live slot, questions submitted in advance
test_cases.input_summary: Suncroft Foods CEO; baby food recall over contamination concern; live radio interview, 10 minutes; three submitted questions: what happened, is it safe now, what are you doing for affected families; key message: proactive recall, no confirmed harm, full refund
test_cases.notes: Live radio format correctly flagged in the context section with practical advice (short sentences, no technical jargon on air). Pre-submitted questions addressed first, then anticipated follow-ups. Fluency note: one suggested response contained a sub-clause that would be difficult to deliver naturally on live radio. Professionalism note: 'safety is our top priority' appeared in one suggested response — flagged as a phrase that sounds hollow; bridging language replaced it on review.

test_cases.description: New CEO first media interview — introductory profile piece, no crisis, brand positioning focus
test_cases.input_summary: Newly appointed CEO of a regional arts organisation; first media interview with arts trade publication; journalist profiling new leadership; key messages: community access, digital expansion, financial sustainability; no sensitive topics; 30-minute print interview
test_cases.notes: Non-crisis briefing note correctly calibrated — less defensive, more conversational tone throughout. Coherence note: the topics-to-avoid section was almost empty (correctly so) but the format created an awkward near-blank section; a revised version would reframe it as 'Areas to Handle With Care' with a lighter touch. Relevance note: included guidance on handling budget questions not mentioned in the input — reasonable anticipation but should be flagged as an assumption.
